Motion by Chair Lynch to allow licensees to extend operating hours from June 11, 2026 through July 19, 2026, during World Cup games, for alcohol service from 9:00 a.m. until 2:00 a.m., and a closing time of no later than 3:00 a.m.
Approval of the minutes of the Licensing Commission Regular Meeting of 3/17/26.
Marinho's Brazilian Grill Corp d/b/a Marinho's Brazilian Grill, 85 Broadway, requesting approval of an all forms alcohol license, common victualer, entertainment by devices, and Sunday hours
Poke SDS LLC d/b/a Pokeworks, 261 Elm Street, requesting approval of a common victualer and entertainment by devices license
Somerville BBQ Inc d/b/a bb.q Chicken, 248 Elm Street, requesting approval of a common victualer and entertainment by devices license
Life Drawing Boston LLC d/b/a Life Drawing Boston, 86 Joy Street, requesting approval of an entertainment by devices, patrons, and performers license

Indignant Brewing Co LLC d/b/a Winter Hill Brewing, 328 Broadway, requesting approval of a new all forms alcohol license to replace their existing Farmer Pourer (Brewery) License (replacing AL17-000125)

Saint Brigid's Well LLC d/b/a Saint Brigid's Well, 525 Medford Street, requesting approval of transfer of an all forms alcohol license from Demeter Hospitality Group d/b/a Daddy Jones and a common victualer, entertainment by devices, 2AM license, and private outdoor dining (transferring from AL17-000059)

Soba Ya LLC d/b/a Sugidama, 234 Elm Street, requesting approval of an alteration of indoor and outdoor premises (amending AL22-000010)

St. Anthony Parish, 12 Properzi Way, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (All Forms) for Baile das Maes (Mother's Day Dance), to be held indoors at 12 Properzi Way, on 5/9/2026 from 8:00 p.m. to 1:00 a.m. (Alcohol Service)

LEGO Discovery Boston, 598 Assembly Row, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (Wine and Malt) for Adult Nights, to be held indoors at 598 Assembly Row, on 7/10/2026, 8/14/2026, 9/11/2026, 10/30/2026, 11/13/2026, and 12/11/2026 from 7:00 p.m. to 9:30 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Dormition of the Virgin Mary, 29 Central Street, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (All Forms) for GyroFest to be held indoors and outdoors at 29 Central Street, on 5/8/2026 and 5/9/2026 (Alcohol Service)

Somerville Museum, 1 Westwood Road, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (All Forms) for Haus Party 003, to be held indoors at Somerville Museum, on 5/15/2026, from 7:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

St. Anthony Parish, 12 Properzi Way, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (All Forms) for Festa Junina 2026, to be held outdoors at 12 Properzi Way parking lot, on 6/20/2026 and 6/21/2026 (Alcohol Service)

Somerville Chamber of Commerce, 561 Windsor Street, requesting approval of a Special Alcohol License (All Forms) for Taste of Somerville, to be held outdoors at Boynton Yards, on 6/10/2026, with a rain date of 6/11/2026, from 5:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Blisspoint Meadery, 1 Fox Run Road, Bedford, requesting approval of a Special Farmer Winery License for Union Square Farmer's Market to be held outdoors at 66-70 Union Square, on Saturdays beginning 5/9/2026 and ending 10/31/2026 from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Far From the Tree LLC, 102 Jackson Street, Salem, requesting approval of a Special Farmer Winery License for Union Square Farmer's Market to be held outdoors at 66-70 Union Square, on Saturdays beginning 5/9/2026 and ending 10/31/2026 from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Blisspoint Meadery, 1 Fox Run Road, Bedford, requesting approval of a Special Farmer Winery License for Assembly Row Farmer's Market to be held outdoors at 375 Assembly Row, on Fridays beginning 6/12/2026 and ending 10/9/2026 from 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Saltbox Cidershop, 22 North Broadway, Haverhill, requesting approval of a Special Farmer Winery License for Assembly Row Farmer's Market to be held outdoors at 375 Assembly Row, on Fridays beginning 6/12/2026 and ending 10/9/2026 from 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. (Alcohol Service)

Call to order with a moment of silence and a salute to the flag of the United States of America.
School Committee Student Advisory Committee Representatives
Public Comment – In person
District Report – Technology update, Enrollment Update, Youth Health Survey
Community or Calendar Items from Committee Members
School Committee Meeting for the Rules Management Subcommittee: March 16, 2026 – Motion to accept the report
School Committee Meeting for the Educational Programs and Instruction Subcommittee: April 2, 2026 – Motion to accept the report
MSBA
Resolution Supporting Inclusive Design for all Somerville School Buildings and Schoolyards (second reading)
Appointments to the Rank Choice Voting Committee and Public Financing of Campaigns Committee (second reading)
UAW (Community School)-Integrated Contract – Motion to authorize the chair to sign the UAW (Community School)-Integrated Contract
Report Out of SC Coordinated Office Hours
SHORE Educational Collaborative Report
Field Trips – May 27–29, 2026: 27 fifth grade students from Brown School to Freedom NH Outdoor Classroom; June 12, 2026: 46 eighth-grade students from Healey School to Canobie Lake Park
Acceptance of FY26 Grants Funds – Harvard Emerging Leaders Program, SHS Mentoring Program $10,000
Upcoming Meeting Topics – May: MTSS, Special Ed Program Review; June: OST Presentation, District Budget, MSBA Plans
Condolence
Adjournment
Introduction and roll call.
Debrief of 90 Washington Street RFP applicant interviews
Review of draft communication to City Council and Redevelopment Authority re. 90 Washington Street proposal review
Public comment
Approval of minutes
Conclusion
Conservation Commission Bylaws
Conservation Agent Action Item Review
Conservation Commission Vacancy / New Commissioners
Incoming Correspondence - Letters & Notifications
Resident Comments / Concerns
New Items / Future Items
Approval of Minutes

That this Council amend its Rule 24 by requiring a majority vote of the Council before a councilor can be removed from a committee.
That the City Clerk work with this Council to amend the Code of Ordinances to better regulate the sale of second-hand goods.
Amending Chapter 8, Article III of the Code of Ordinances pertaining to secondhand dealer licenses.
That the Code of Ordinances be amended to prohibit the use of tax-payer funds for self-promotion.
Amending Chapter 2, Article III of the Code of Ordinances by adding a new section establishing the time and place of regular meetings of the City Council.
That Rule 4 of the Rules of the City Council be amended to update submission deadlines for agenda items, consistent with the provisions of the Open Meeting Law.
